title: Bricks are falling from a smokestack at historic former Syracuse train station. City orders it razed
summary: The City of Syracuse has ordered demolition of a 100-foot smokestack at the historic former New York Central Railroad building after bricks began falling onto public areas since April. The decision follows a dispute between the city's Code Enforcement division and property owner Tom Hornstein over responsibility for repairs; Hornstein attributes deterioration to nearby DOT construction vibrations and estimates demolition costs at $60,000, while the DOT and city attribute the damage to inadequate maintenance.
department_refs: New York State Department of Transportation

political_signal.description: Code enforcement action against private property owner with escalation to mandatory demolition; underlying dispute over responsibility allocation between private developer, city, and state agency; potential financial and historic preservation implications
